metadata.description: Normalized job title registry for Saudi Career Ops. Covers roles common in the Saudi market with Arabic and English variants. Seniority is a hint derived from common usage patterns — it is not authoritative and should be combined with CV evidence.
metadata.notes: The canonical_name is English. Arabic variants reflect how the title typically appears in Saudi Arabic-language job postings — they may differ from direct translations. Tamheer variants are listed separately due to their distinct employment classification.

job_titles.notes: High-volume role in Saudi Arabia, particularly in Vision 2030-linked entities. Often requires government relationship experience alongside commercial skills. 'Head of BD' maps to senior-manager or director level in most Saudi organizations.
Entry-to-mid level. Typically supports a BD Manager with market research, proposal preparation, and CRM management.
Entry-to-junior level in investment firms and sovereign wealth vehicles. CFA Level 1+ is a common screening criterion. Financial modeling proficiency is a baseline expectation.

Senior role in the Saudi investment ecosystem. CFA charterholder status is frequently required. Cross-sector exposure to Vision 2030 themes (tourism, entertainment, industrial) is increasingly valued.

Saudi-specific role with high demand. Involves navigating licensing requirements across government portals (Balady, Qiwa, MISA, municipality systems). Arabic language proficiency is typically required.

One of the highest-volume role categories in Saudi Arabia given the scale of Vision 2030 infrastructure programs. PMP certification is a common requirement. Arabic is often required for government-adjacent projects. Scope varies enormously — from small IT projects to multi-billion-riyal megaprojects.
Entry-to-junior level support under a Project Manager. Common as a Tamheer placement role for Saudi national graduates.

Growing demand across Saudi sectors. Mid-level roles typically require SQL, Excel, and Power BI. Python or R usage varies by organization maturity. Arabic-language reporting capability is valued in government and semi-government contexts.
Senior level implies ownership of analytical frameworks and mentoring junior analysts. Python proficiency and dashboard ownership are common expectations.

In Saudi Arabia, Saudization compliance tracking and Qiwa platform management are standard responsibilities at this level. GOSI registration and HRDF claims experience is frequently required. CIPD or equivalent is a positive signal.
Senior HR role requiring strategic partnership with business leaders. More common in multinational and PIF ecosystem entities than in traditional Saudi private sector companies.

Entry-to-junior level in strategy functions. High demand in PIF entities and government transformation programs. Consulting-style analytical skills (structuring problems, slide decks, Excel models) are baseline expectations.

Tamheer is a distinct employment classification, not a standard internship. Compensation is subsidized by HRDF. Duration is fixed (up to 12 months). Tag with employment_type: tamheer — not full_time or internship.
Administrative track Tamheer placement. Common in government-adjacent entities and large corporates.
Finance track Tamheer placement. Typically involves accounts payable/receivable, budgeting support, and financial reporting under supervision.
IT track Tamheer placement. Common in telecom companies and large technology departments of Saudi corporates.
